denial_pattern_sources: wrong_appeals_contact: State Insurance Commissioner complaint logs (CA, NY, TX) 2023
ignored_physician_letter: AMA 2023 Prior Authorization Physician Survey
denial_letter_text:
Dear Member,

We received your provider's request on 02/14/2026 for Intensive outpatient program (IOP) for depression related to Major depressive disorder, recurrent, moderate (F33.1). After review, we cannot approve this request.

Our review applied Aetna Clinical Policy Bulletin (CPB) criteria and InterQual review standards. The documentation submitted does not demonstrate that the service is medically necessary for this member at this time. Emergency imaging/treatment for Major depressive disorder, recurrent, moderate (F33.1) is denied for lack of prior authorization. Retroactive authorization is not granted for non-emergent services.

Specifically, the information provided does not satisfy the utilization-management requirements associated with emergency retroactive auth. The clinical notes reviewed did not include sufficient detail to confirm that plan criteria were met. The requesting provider may submit additional records, but the current file does not support approval.

If your provider wishes to discuss this determination, they may contact our physician review line. Any discussion must occur within the timeframe described in your plan materials.

You have the right to appeal this determination. To file a standard appeal, send a written request and supporting medical records within 180 days of the date of this letter. Include your member identification number and the service reference shown on your explanation of benefits.

Sincerely,

Aetna Medical Management

Standard criteria apply regardless of information previously sent to the plan.
clinical_context: The 34-year-old member has Major depressive disorder, recurrent, moderate (F33.1). He has been treated under commercial coverage with Intensive outpatient program (IOP) for depression recommended as the next appropriate step. PHQ-9 scores 19 and 21 in Q4 2025 and Q1 2026; failed sertraline 200 mg ×14 weeks and bupropion XL 300 mg ×12 weeks. This directly contradicts the insurer's position that emergency retroactive auth bars coverage: the chart documents dates, doses, and objective findings that were submitted with the original request but are not reflected in the denial letter's rationale. Treating clinicians note ongoing functional impairment and avoidable harm if his care is delayed further.

scenario_realism.reasoning: Diagnosis, prior treatments, and insurer administrative posture reflect everyday commercial UM denials rather than textbook perfection.

insurer_voice.reasoning: Letter reads as authentic Aetna correspondence with CPB and InterQual fingerprints and procedural tone.
